parameters.name: inputgain
parameters.label: Input Gain (dB)
parameters.description: This parameter controls the volume of the channel before it reaches the compressor.  If the signal to be compressed is not in a useful dynamic range, this parameter can be used to repair it.

parameters.name: enablecompressor
parameters.label: Enable Compressor
parameters.description: Turns the compressor on or off.

parameters.name: compressiontype
parameters.label: Compression Type
parameters.description: Determines which compression method to use.

parameters.name: chanlinkingcomp
parameters.label: Channel Linking
parameters.description: As various channels come into the , they can either be compressed by an equal amount, or individually.  If they are compressed equally, all of the channels will be evaluated for the highest peak value

parameters.name: thresholdcompressor
parameters.label: Threshold (dB)
parameters.description: This parameter sets the threshold value which a signal must cross before compression is applied. It uses a decibel scale, where '0 decibels' would be considered the loudest possible signal*, and '-60

parameters.name: ratiocompressor
parameters.label: Ratio
parameters.description: The ratio is the amount of compression that will be applied to the signal, with respect to how far the signal has gone past the threshold value. A ratio of '0' will apply no compression. A value of '1

parameters.name: kneecompressor
parameters.label: Knee
parameters.description: The knee defines how the will transition into compression as signals approach or cross the threshold. With a knee of '0' (a hard knee), think of the compressor as applying a flat compression response,

parameters.name: attackcompressor
parameters.label: Attack (msec=10**val)
parameters.description: The attack will control how quickly the compressor responds when a signal crosses the threshold.  Increasing the attack parameter will cause the compressor to apply compression at a slower and smoothe

parameters.name: releasecompressor
parameters.label: Release (msec=10**val)
parameters.description: The release will control how quickly the compressor responds when a signal drops to a lower level, or goes below the threshold altogether. Just like the attack, higher value will slow down the respons

parameters.name: gaincompressor
parameters.label: Output Gain (dB)
parameters.description: After applying compression, the signal can be reduced with Gain to a lower volume level. To make up the lost volume, this parameter can be increased.

parameters.name: enablelimiter
parameters.label: Enable Limiter
parameters.description: Turns the limiter on or off.

parameters.name: chanlinkinglim
parameters.label: Channel Linking
parameters.description: Same as compressor.

parameters.name: thresholdlimiter
parameters.label: Threshold (dB)
parameters.description: This is the threshold value which a signal must cross before limiting is applied. Usually, this value should be left at '0' decibels. Just like the compressor, a value of '0' decibels is considered to

parameters.name: releaselimiter
parameters.label: Release (msec=10**val)
parameters.description: Although the attack of a limiter is always quick, the release can still be set by the user. This will determine how long the limiter takes to transition out of a limiting situation. Increasing the rel

parameters.name: kneelimiter
parameters.label: Knee
parameters.description: Similar to the compressor, this parameter controls how the will transition into limiting, when a signal becomes louder. A larger knee will mean a smoother transition. See the Knee diagram above.

parameters.name: drywet
parameters.label: Dry / Wet Mix
parameters.description: As this parameter is reduced from 1 (Wet) toward 0 (Dry), it removes the effect of the filter.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).
